content:
Everyone should read this article:
What has happened to the world? And in what age are we living? This is an age of hardship, people.
We are now at a point where one can get whatever they want, yet despite all the science, inventions, and all means of communication, artificial intelligence, landing on the moon, space exploration, splitting the atom, laser surgery, embryo implantation, genetic engineering, and the wonders of AI… we have advanced — we have gained much, this is true. But what we have lost is far greater! We have lost nobility, humanity, love, gentleness, simplicity, chivalry, beauty, elegance, and cleanliness.
And the lingering question is:
All the means of true comfort are now available to us. Yet human peace of mind is lacking — and why?
Homes have become more equipped.
Means of communication have become faster.
Information has become more accessible.
Opportunities for learning and work have become broader.
Yet still, many people complain of anxiety, tightness in the chest, and stress — as if tranquility has become something rare.
So what is the reason?
Man was created with two integrated souls: a soul that needs material means, and a soul that needs meaning.
A soul that needs sustenance from the heavens, and a soul that needs sustenance from the earth.
On earth, we get our food, but we lack the nourishment from the heavens — which is spiritual nourishment.
Our era has succeeded greatly in improving material means, but it has not succeeded equally in feeding meaning.
People learn how to increase their income.
But few learn how to increase their inner serenity and peace of mind. They plan for their professional future...
but they do not plan for their afterlife,
nor do they plan for their relationship with God, nor for building their hearts.
And from here, the imbalance begins.
Then came social media, and man began comparing his life every day to the hundreds of people he sees on these platforms.
He compares his home to their homes.
His provision to their provisions.
His children to their children.
His travels to their travels.
His success to their success.
And constant comparison does not allow the heart to rest, because it makes him feel that what he has is less than what others have — even if he possesses blessings that millions of people would dream of.
Among the reasons, too, is that many people have started consuming news more than they consume the Qur'an.
They know the details of crises around the world...
but they do not give their hearts enough minutes to be nourished by the words of God.
That is why He, Glorified be He, said: "Verily, in the remembrance of God do hearts find rest." (Qur'an 13:28)
Tranquility is not the fruit of having no problems; rather, it is the fruit of God's presence in the heart.
A person may live in material hardship, yet be at peace.
And another may live in abundance, yet know no serenity.
Not because the first has more means, but because he has a heart more connected to God.
Moreover, a balanced life has become less present.
Work competes with family.
The phone competes with conversation.
Speed competes with reflection.
Many people now spend their entire day reacting to things, rather than choosing what deserves their time and attention.
And a person does not regain peace merely by circumstances changing...
The secret of anxiety is that we live without religion... without faith... and that our religiosity is only outward — words on our tongues during occasions, and prayers performed out of habit.
Anxiety is a deeply spiritual ailment. Its cause is the lack of meaning in life.
Scientific and material superiority has not been accompanied by spiritual superiority.
We have become giants in our tools and machines — cars, airplanes, rockets, satellites — but we remain dwarfs in our wisdom.
We have matter, but we do not know how to handle it properly.
We have muscles, but we lack character.
We have knowledge, but we lack love.
Rather, peace comes when a person rearranges their priorities.
When they realize that success does not replace serenity.
That wealth does not replace contentment.
That a large number of followers does not replace a sincere relationship with God.
Tranquility is not a gift that the world grants to anyone... rather, it is the fruit of a heart that has known its Lord, accepted His decree, made good use of material means, and did not make the world its greatest concern.
That is why one of the greatest supplications that the Prophet ﷺ used to repeat was:
"O God, set right for me my religion, which is the safeguard of my affairs; and set right for me my worldly life, in which is my livelihood..."
For when religion is set right, a person's perspective on the world becomes upright, and when their perspective becomes upright, they find a peace that neither abundance of wealth nor plenty of means can grant.
Try not to gloat, not to hate, not to hold grudges, not to envy, not to despair, and not to be pessimistic — and you will see the astonishing result for yourself. You will see that you can indeed be healed from your ailments. It is a difficult journey.
It will require from you continuous and persistent struggles with your own self, perhaps for years and years, and it will require that you remain in a declared war against your selfishness and greed — a war in which reason, resolve, faith, determination, patience, perseverance, and willpower all take part.
